citations.summary: Standard ultralocal cluster-expansion theorem with exact smallness threshold, local influence and the 3*kappa0+3 decay loss.
extracted_claims: c0 and O(1) depend only on the dimension.
H#(Y) depends only on H(X) for X contained in Y.

formulas.statement: Input activity decay.
Output connected activity decay.

use_for: Source anchor for the ordinary ultralocal cluster expansion used by Dimock I and referenced by Dimock II Appendix F.
do_not_use_for: Do not silently replace the with-holes metric or Ω-connectivity by the ordinary theorem.
open_questions: Complete theorem-fed connected-cover entropy constants in the repository finite setting.

citations.summary: Lemma 28 resolves C_{k,r} into local algebraic factors and a Green operator, the identity later localized by random-walk expansions.

formulas.statement: Resolvent decomposition.
formulas.ascii: C_(k,r) = A_(k,r) + a_k^2 * A_(k,r) * Q_k * G_(k,r) * Q_k^T * A_(k,r)

formulas.statement: Definition of the Green operator in Lemma 28.

use_for: Algebraic source map for covariance/root localization by Green-function expansion.
do_not_use_for: Do not identify scalar Q_k or Delta_k with the gauge Hessian without the Wilson-Hessian theorem.
open_questions: Exact physical-gauge operator dictionary and boundary conditions.

citations.summary: Exact small-field minimizer expansion, fluctuation covariance and one-step Gaussian normalization/scaling formulas.
extracted_claims: Lemma 4 expands the density around the minimizer and isolates a centered Gaussian with covariance C_k.
Lemma 5 scales the density to the next unit lattice and updates the global normalization Z_{k+1}.

formulas.statement: Fluctuation covariance after expansion at the minimizer.
formulas.conclusion: Defines the Gaussian covariance in Eq. (66).

formulas.statement: One-step scaled normalization factor.

dictionary_links.statement: Scalar fluctuation covariance corresponding to the Gaussian-root interface.

use_for: Normalization/Jacobian checklist for a localized Gaussian RG step.
do_not_use_for: Do not copy scalar determinant or field-space dimensions into Yang-Mills without proof.
open_questions: Exact finite-dimensional coordinate/Jacobian dictionary for the repository physical gauge field.

citations.summary: Uniform summability of connected M-polymers pinned at one block, by size and by tree distance.
extracted_claims: The bounds are dimension-dependent combinatorial constants and are uniform in the pinning block.
The d_M bound is the geometric substrate used by the standard cluster expansion.

formulas.statement: Pinned polymer summability by block cardinality.
formulas.ascii: sum_{X: X contains Box} exp(-a*|X|_M) <= b

formulas.statement: Pinned polymer summability by tree distance.
formulas.ascii: sum_{X: X contains Box} exp(-kappa_0*d_M(X)) <= K_0

use_for: Combinatorial source for rooted/pinned exponential polymer sums.
do_not_use_for: Do not reuse d_M as the modified metric d_M(-, mod Omega^c) without Lemma E.3 or a comparison theorem.
open_questions: Exact dimension dependence of a,b,kappa_0,K_0 if numeric constants become necessary.

citations.summary: Random-walk expansion of G_k and uniform exponential block-distance estimates for G_k and differentiated variants.
extracted_claims: For M sufficiently large, the random-walk series for G_k, partial G_k, and delta_alpha partial G_k converge.
The bounds are uniform in the block labels and decay as exp(-(gamma_0/2)d(y,y')).

formulas.statement: Green-function random-walk expansion.
formulas.conclusion: Decomposes the Green operator into localized walk contributions.

formulas.statement: Block-distance estimates for the Green operator and derivatives.
formulas.ascii: |(G_k f)(x)|, |(partial G_k f)(x)|, |(delta_alpha partial G_k f)(x,x')| <= C*exp(-(gamma_0/2)*d(y,y'))*||f||_infty
formulas.conclusion: Uniform exponential localization in the block metric.

use_for: Source shape for Green/covariance localization and random-walk reconstruction.
do_not_use_for: Do not identify the scalar G_k with the physical Yang-Mills C_k or its root without the rescaling and field dictionary.
open_questions: Exact transfer of the block metric and differentiated estimates to physical bonds/cubes.

citations.summary: Polymer counting and metric-weight summability around a pinned cube or an intersected target polymer.
extracted_claims: A connected-polymer counting estimate gives a finite pinned-cube exponential sum when a>2d log 2.
The metric inequality d(X)>=3^(-d)|X|-1 transfers this to the d_M-weighted bound.

formulas.statement: Pinned-cube metric summability.
Target-intersection volume bounds.
formulas.ascii: sum_{X: X intersect Y != empty} exp(-a*|X|_M) <= b*|Y|_M; sum_{X: X intersect Y != empty} exp(-kappa0*d_M(X)) <= K0*|Y|_M
formulas.conclusion: Local geometric summability proportional to target volume.

use_for: Geometric substrate for cluster-expansion root sums and local KP windows.
do_not_use_for: Do not identify d_M with the repository discreteModifiedMetric without the metric dictionary.
open_questions: Dimension/adjacency normalization in the exact repository polymer type.

citations.summary: Bibliographic spine locating the main Balaban lattice-gauge papers and Cammarota CMP85 from one checked primary reference list.
extracted_claims: CMP95: Propagators and renormalization transformations for lattice gauge field theories I, CMP 95, 17-40 (1984).
CMP96: Propagators and renormalization transformations for lattice gauge field theories II, CMP 96, 223-250 (1984).
CMP98: Averaging operators for lattice gauge field theories, CMP 98, 17-51 (1985).
CMP99: Propagators for lattice gauge field theories in a background field III, CMP 99, 389-434 (1985).
CMP109, CMP116, CMP119, CMP122-I and CMP122-II are listed as the main RG/large-field sequence.
Reference [33] identifies Cammarota, Decay of correlations for infinite range interactions in unbounded spin systems, CMP 85, 517-528 (1982).
use_for: Stable bibliographic cross-check before opening the individual primary papers.
do_not_use_for: Do not treat bibliography entries as theorem extraction or formula verification.
open_questions: Add DOI and local-artifact hashes for CMP95, CMP96, CMP98 and CMP99 after obtaining primary copies.

citations.summary: Lemma 21 turns the localized small-field fluctuation integral into an exponential of local activities with explicit scale and metric decay.

formulas.statement: Local exponential representation of the fluctuation integral.
Decay of the cluster-expanded small-field activity.

use_for: Architecture and loss bookkeeping for localization followed by cluster expansion.
do_not_use_for: This is a three-dimensional scalar small-field theorem, not a Yang-Mills activity theorem.
open_questions: Model-specific gauge analogue and support dictionary.

citations.summary: Exact proof-stage chain from Ω-local geometry through metric stitching to the first activity K(Y) and its ultralocal integral K#(Y).

formulas.statement: Modified-metric stitching for an Ω-connected family.
formulas.ascii: d_M(Y, mod Omega^c) <= sum_i d_M(X_i, mod Omega^c) + (n-1)
assumptions: the family {X_i} has n elements and is Ω-connected

formulas.statement: First connected activity bound after cover entropy and metric stitching.
Ultralocal integration defining K#.

use_for: Exact source target for the current connected-cover entropy and ultralocal integration frontier.
do_not_use_for: The phrase “with some further analysis” does not expose the missing entropy constants; Lean must prove or separately source them.
open_questions: Close the finite connected-cover entropy sum producing Eq. (642).
Build measurable K and K# for the physical field types.

citations.summary: Second Ursell exponentiation defining H# from K#, with the Ω-connected Ursell coefficient and exact union constraint.

formulas.statement: Definition of the final connected activity H#.
formulas.ascii: H#(Y) = sum_{n>=1} 1/n! * sum_{(Y1,...,Yn): union_i Yi=Y} rho^T(Y1,...,Yn) * product_i K#(Yi)
assumptions: rho^T vanishes when the Y_i are not Ω-connected

use_for: Exact source definition of the second connected/Ursell activity and its union fiber.
do_not_use_for: Do not replace the infinite source series by the finite Lean series without the limiting/dominating argument required by the chosen interface.
open_questions: Match the source Ursell coefficient convention to the finite Lean coefficient exactly.
Complete the final summability estimate yielding Eq. (636).

citations.summary: Lemma 3.18 applies Appendix F to the localized large-field fluctuation integral, with explicit t/u radii, source input bound and final metric loss.

formulas.statement: Cluster-expanded large-field fluctuation integral.
formulas.ascii: Xi''_(k,Pi+)(t,u) = exp(sum_{Y intersect Lambda_(k+1) != empty} H#_(k,Pi+)(t,u,Y))

formulas.statement: Output decay from Lemma 3.18.
The assembled activity bound used as Appendix-F input.
assumptions: lambda_k sufficiently small and the component bounds in Eq. (505)

use_for: Concrete example of how component estimates are assembled into Appendix-F input and lose another 3*kappa0+3.
do_not_use_for: Do not identify t/u deformation parameters or scalar boundary terms with the CMP116 D/P/Z0/Z0-prime indices.
open_questions: Gauge-specific termwise bound and exact physical activity construction.

citations.summary: Section 3.8 defines the restricted fluctuation covariance, the nonlocal square-root change of variables and the resolvent/random-walk localization interface.
extracted_claims: The formal square-root change gives an ultralocal identity-covariance Gaussian but is too nonlocal inside characteristic functions.
The paper therefore replaces the square root by a localized approximation built from resolvents and weakened Green functions.

formulas.statement: Localized-region fluctuation covariance.
Integral representation of the covariance square root.
assumptions: C'_(k,Omega) positive/invertible in the finite setting

formulas.statement: Restricted resolvent identity feeding random-walk localization.
assumptions: Definitions of A_(k,r) and G prime_(k,Omega,r) in Eqs. (274)-(275)

dictionary_links.statement: Localized covariance-root map used to push the fluctuation Gaussian to identity covariance.
dictionary_links.blocker: Exact gauge Hessian, domain, determinant and local-piece reconstruction.

use_for: Exact source architecture for covariance-root localization and ultralocal Gaussianization.
do_not_use_for: The exact scalar resolvent identity is not a Wilson-gauge Hessian identity.
open_questions: Exact source-to-Lean coordinate map and determinant normalization.
Convergence/reconstruction theorem for the localized root pieces.

citations.summary: Removal and localization of boundary-dependent terms using local influence and a Cauchy contour representation.
extracted_claims: Boundary activities vanish for Y contained in Lambda_{k+1} by local influence.
The contour in Eq. (510) uses the allowed u-radius from Lemma 3.18.
The localized boundary activity inherits the kappa-6*kappa_0-6 exponent.

formulas.statement: Factorization of boundary and non-boundary contributions.
Decay of the localized boundary activity.
assumptions: The analyticity domain and Lemma 3.18 output
formulas.conclusion: Provides a localized decaying boundary activity.

formulas.statement: Cauchy representation of the boundary difference.
formulas.conclusion: Transfers the analytic H# bound to B#.

use_for: Source anchor for extracting boundary activities without losing locality.
do_not_use_for: Do not interpret the relation Y # Lambda as ordinary set-theoretic disjointness without the paper convention.
open_questions: Repository definition matching the source boundary-touching relation.

citations.summary: Uniform pinned summability for polymers with holes in the modified metric d_M(-, mod Omega^c).
extracted_claims: Omega is a union of M-cubes and the pinning block lies in Omega.
The proof compares the modified metric with the tree length of X intersect Omega and counts possible hole components.
The final invocation of Lemma E.2 requires kappa_0 > 4a in the proof.

formulas.statement: Pinned modified-metric summability.
formulas.ascii: sum_{X in D_k(mod Omega^c), X contains Box} exp(-kappa_0*d_M(X, mod Omega^c)) <= K_0
assumptions: Omega is a union of M-cubes
formulas.conclusion: Gives a volume-uniform root sum for polymers with holes.

formulas.statement: Reduction to the active-region tree length.
formulas.ascii: d_M(X, mod Omega^c) >= tilde_ell_M(X intersect Omega) >= (1/2)*ell_M(X intersect Omega)
formulas.conclusion: Reduces modified-metric summability to an ordinary tree-counting estimate.

use_for: Geometric substrate for the volume-uniform cluster expansion with holes.
do_not_use_for: Do not replace the modified metric by ordinary d_M without recording the comparison.
open_questions: Exact correspondence between X intersect Omega and the repository skeleton H X.

citations.summary: Theorem 3.1 states the multiscale large/small-field representation and repeats the exact global normalization recursion under explicit large-L, large-M and small-coupling conditions.

formulas.statement: Global normalization in the large-field representation.
assumptions: M sufficiently large depending on L
lambda_k sufficiently small depending on L,M
epsilon_k and mu_k selected in Part I

use_for: Exact assumptions and normalization factor for the Dimock large-field induction.
do_not_use_for: Do not transfer scalar counterterms or determinants directly to four-dimensional gauge fields.
open_questions: Gauge-field analogue of the multiscale representation (201)-(202).

citations.summary: Final cluster activity estimate and stability corollary, uniform in the ultraviolet cutoff and volume index.

formulas.statement: Final exponentiated polymer activity bound.
Relative partition-function stability.
assumptions: some eta>0 independent of M,N; proof takes eta=beta/2

use_for: End-to-end scalar RG sanity check connecting local polymer estimates to a volume-uniform stability bound.
do_not_use_for: A stability bound is not a Yang-Mills mass gap, continuum construction or spectral-gap theorem.
open_questions: No direct Lean consumer is claimed for the final scalar stability inequality.

citations.summary: Theorem 1 collects the convergent scalar RG representation and the exact local E, R and active-boundary B decay estimates.
extracted_claims: The theorem assumes 0<lambda<e^-1, 0<mu_bar<=1, L sufficiently large, M sufficiently large depending on L, and lambda_k sufficiently small depending on L,M.
O(1) is independent of all parameters, while C may depend on L; B0 depends on L and M.

formulas.statement: Main local correction bound.
Tiny local remainder bound.
Active boundary activity bound with holes.
assumptions: X intersects both Lambda_k and its complement

use_for: Source-faithful architectural target showing distinct regular, remainder and active-boundary local activities.
do_not_use_for: These are scalar phi^4 bounds and do not prove Yang-Mills hRpoly.
Do not collapse lambda_k^n0 or metric decay into a bare geometric scalar remainder without a summation theorem.
open_questions: Concrete Yang-Mills analogues and source-to-Lean polymer/support dictionary.

description: Source-extracted Dimock RG I-III batch, extended with random-walk Green bounds, modified-metric summability, boundary removal, and a checked Balaban bibliography spine.
